hypotheses.label: MOpt's corpus reaches the gate keyword but drives the Lemon parser shallower per seed than naive's, never iterating deep enough to fire the reduce

params.note: runs the resolving (naive-side, winner) vs blocking (mopt-side, loser) seeds through the cov image; reads per-seed-normalized execution count + blocked-side count at yy_reduce:157670

hypotheses.prediction: naive's winner seeds drive yy_reduce:157670 MEASURABLY DEEPER per seed than mopt's blocker seeds (winner_deeper=true, depth_lift high), AND the blocked true-side count is reached by the winner corpus but not the loser corpus (side_lift high). The card observes naive seeds fire the true side (T=9) and exercise the reduce path hundreds of times while mopt seeds bail early at T=0. This rules out the alternative that BOTH corpora drive the parser equally deep and the divergence is a pure terminal-resolve artifact (under which depth_lift ~= 1, winner_deeper=false). It is a genuinely different mechanism from the refuted keyword-assembly story: the prior token_count test did NOT find 'SELECT' depleted in mopt's corpus, so the deficit is not keyword PRESENCE but parse DEPTH / reduce-iteration count behind the keyword.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: rules out the null that mopt and naive corpora reach the reduce action at equal per-seed depth (depth_lift ~= 1, winner_deeper=false) — i.e. that the resolve/block split is not reflected in how deep each corpus actually drives the parser. A falsified prediction (mopt seeds reach yy_reduce as deep as naive's) would say the depth-deficit story is wrong and the mechanism is non-discriminable here.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach reads the per-seed-normalized llvm-cov execution count of the branch line from a NEUTRAL cov binary — it measures how deep each fuzzer's CORPUS drives the parser, not whether the campaign labeled the branch resolved/blocked. The resolve label was assigned by the >=8/8 W/L campaign rule; this signal is the independent corpus-trajectory measurement (G2).

hypotheses.label: MOpt operator scheduling under-assembles the gate keyword that naive's havoc reaches

params.scope: saved corpus seeds for the branch (sqlite3 has on-disk corpus)
params.token_lift_def: median naive_tokens / max(median mopt_tokens, epsilon) at the gate keyword

hypotheses.prediction: mopt's saved corpus contains FEWER occurrences of the gate keyword 'SELECT' (plus its long-expression body) than naive's, i.e. mopt_tokens < naive_tokens. If MOpt's scheduling truly underweights the token-insert/replace operators needed to assemble the keyword, the keyword should be depleted in mopt's corpus relative to naive's; an alternative in which both reach the keyword equally (scheduling irrelevant) predicts mopt_tokens ~= naive_tokens.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: the null that MOpt scheduling does not affect keyword assembly (mopt and naive corpora carry the keyword at parity); under that null token_lift ~= 1.
hypotheses.g2_independence: token_count over saved corpus BYTE content is independent of the resolve/block label that defined the shape — it measures what each fuzzer's corpus contains, not whether the blocker branch fired. It does not 'verify mopt-blocks with mopt-blocks'.

hypotheses.label: The ROOT mechanism — MOpt's particle-swarm operator-scheduling DISTRIBUTION being worse for this structured-SQL goal — is non-observable in the available campaign data; no remaining built tool discriminates it from a generic single-trial corpus-trajectory difference

params.would_require: per-operator selection-probability / scheduling trace per fuzzer, which does NOT exist: the havoc+token operator VOCABULARY is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers and MOpt's particle-swarm scheduling is a probability DISTRIBUTION over that shared vocabulary, leaving no mutation_op trace (data_reality #1). value_profile-style admission events are also absent. No seed_lineage descriptor can recover the scheduling cause.

untried_tools_assessed.operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio: FORBIDDEN — state_or_grammar gate with variable-size winner/loser seeds; byte-offset alignment fails (data_reality #3); returns no_gate_signature.
untried_tools_assessed.value_distance_reached: non-discriminating — the gate operand 'SELECT' is already PRESENT in mopt's corpus (token_count refuted depletion), so Hamming distance to the operand is ~0 for BOTH arms; nothing to separate.
untried_tools_assessed.corpus_size_ratio: non-discriminating for G1 — a global corpus-size/diversity gap predicts the SAME direction for essentially every mopt-anti branch regardless of this gate's structure (SELECT present + per-seed depth parity), so it cannot rule out the obvious alternative; it confounds throughput with the scheduling-assembly claim rather than isolating it.

hypotheses.prediction: No falsifiable, discriminating real-data prediction exists for the SCHEDULING-attribution claim itself. The card's own weakest_evidence_point concedes the scheduling story is INFERRED from operator-set parity plus seed structure, not measured. After the two consequence-proxies above (keyword PRESENCE and per-seed parse DEPTH), the remaining built tools (value_distance_reached, corpus_size_ratio) measure quantities that are either already equal across arms or non-discriminating for this gate, and operand_enrichment is forbidden by data_reality #3. The scheduling cause is therefore non-observable.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: n/a — declared non-discriminable rather than tested. Forcing corpus_size_ratio or value_distance_reached here would violate G1 (the rule could not distinguish the scheduling mechanism from a generic corpus-trajectory difference) and would over-claim a label the data cannot support.

data_realities_respected.no_mutation_op_lineage: MOpt scheduling (a probability distribution over operators) is INVISIBLE in mutation_op — the havoc/token vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers (data_reality #1). NO hypothesis proposes a seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'MOpt scheduling event' as a scorable test; the third hypothesis names that descriptor only to DECLARE it infeasible and mark the root-cause mechanism decidable:false.
data_realities_respected.corpus_available: sqlite3 is one of the 4 on-disk-corpus targets (data_reality #4), and the branch has resolving + blocking seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (data_reality #5), so both the seed_set token_count and seed_set depth_reach descriptors can score this branch.
data_realities_respected.alignment_safe: this is a state_or_grammar gate with variable-size winner/loser seeds (data_reality #3), so byte-offset alignment is NOT assumed: operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio is deliberately NOT used (it would return no_gate_signature on a structural gate). token_count is substring occurrence; depth_reach is per-seed execution count — neither assumes a fixed byte offset.
data_realities_respected.cov_image_present: depth_reach needs the libafl-sqlite3-cov image (present) and runs Docker per-branch (~30-60s); scope_limits satisfied.
data_realities_respected.untried_tools_ruled_out: value_distance_reached (operand already present in both arms => distance ~0, non-discriminating), corpus_size_ratio (same-direction for all mopt-anti branches => not discriminating, fails G1), operand_enrichment (forbidden, data_reality #3). None yields a genuinely-different DISCRIMINATING mechanism for this branch — see hypothesis #3.

notes: Target sqlite3, single branch (sqlite3_14748), gate yy_reduce:157670 — input must begin with 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) plus a long well-formed expression body to reach the deep Lemon-parser reduce. SUPERSET/MONOTONIC: both prior hypotheses are KEPT verbatim — mopt_shallow_parse_depth_deficit (depth_reach) and mopt_scheduling_keyword_assembly_deficit (token_count) — unchanged in measurement and rule, in decision_order, so any branch the arbiter can still validate re-validates. PRIOR OUTCOMES (round state: 1/2 rules scored, none held): round-0 token_count was rule_not_met ('SELECT' was NOT depleted in mopt's corpus — mopt DOES carry the keyword), and round-1 depth_reach did not hold either (no per-seed parse-depth gap). Both CONSEQUENCE proxies have now failed. NEW for round-2: rather than relax a refuted threshold (forbidden) or force a non-discriminating tool, I add hypothesis #3 mopt_scheduling_root_cause_nonobservable marked decidable:false (G3). RATIONALE: the genuinely-different remaining mechanism is the SCHEDULING ROOT CAUSE itself (MOpt's particle-swarm operator-selection distribution), and data_reality #1 makes it non-observable — the operator vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers and scheduling leaves no mutation_op trace. I explicitly assessed all three untried built tools: operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio is FORBIDDEN (state_or_grammar gate, variable-size seeds, data_reality #3); value_distance_reached is non-discriminating because the operand 'SELECT' is already present in mopt's corpus so Hamming distance is ~0 for both arms; corpus_size_ratio fails G1 because a global corpus-size gap predicts the same direction for every mopt-anti branch and cannot isolate THIS gate's scheduling-assembly claim. mopt_mutation is a NON-LOCAL adaptive-scheduling family (per Rule 3, legitimately often non-discriminable). Per G3, an honest inconclusive is the CORRECT outcome — I do not invent a mechanism to force a label. If neither KEPT rule holds on re-score, the branch falls to fallback:inconclusive, which is the truthful verdict for a scheduling cause that terminal-corpus tools cannot attribute at n=1.
